rationale: US-Iran conflict continues with second consecutive day of mutual strikes and Trump's renewed Kharg Island threat — qualitative continuation of last cycle's escalation, keeping energy price risk for Poland elevated, though no new step-change beyond what was already reset.

top_events.title: US and Iran exchange strikes for second consecutive day
top_events.summary: US and Iranian forces struck each other across the Middle East for a second day running, with Bahrain releasing footage of intercepted Iranian drones. Trump also reiterated the US 'will be taking' Kharg Island. This is a continuation of the escalation already flagged last cycle — Hormuz remains closed, energy price risk for Poland persists, but no new qualitative leap beyond yesterday's reset.

top_events.title: Hazardous materials incident at Pentagon
top_events.summary: Firefighters responded to a hazardous materials incident at the Pentagon. Details remain sparse — if this is linked to the US-Iran conflict it could signal escalation, but currently appears to be an isolated domestic incident with no confirmed connection to hostilities and minimal direct impact on Poland.

top_events.title: Powerful earthquake kills 37 in southern Philippines
top_events.summary: A strong earthquake struck the southern Philippines, leaving at least 37 dead. A regional tragedy with no meaningful direct or indirect impact on Poland's security, economy or daily life.
